title: `vvp` output is not fully redirected
body:
When using a terminal, it is not possible to completely redirect/suppress the output of `vvp`.

Module and testbench example:

```systemverilog
module example (
  input logic [7:0] d,
  output logic [3:0] q
);
  always_comb begin
    q = d[3:0];
  end
endmodule

module testbench;
  logic [7:0] d;
  logic [3:0] q;

  example example_instance (.d(d), .q(q));

  initial begin
    d = 255;
    #0;
    $display(q);
  end
endmodule
```

Here are the commands without redirection:

```bash
iverilog -g2012 out.sv -oout.vvp; vvp out.vvp
```

And here is their result:

```text
out.sv:5: sorry: constant selects in always_* processes are not currently supported (all bits will be included).
15
```

And here are the commands with redirection `stdout` and `stderr` to `/dev/null`:

```bash
iverilog -g2012 out.sv -oout.vvp; vvp out.vvp 2>&1 > /dev/null
```

You can see that the output of the `$display` command is gone, but the notice `sorry: ...` remains:

```text
out.sv:5: sorry: constant selects in always_* processes are not currently supported (all bits will be included).
```

**The expected behavior is the absence of any output in the console after executing the commands (for this example).**

Version: `Icarus Verilog version 12.0 (stable)`
code_blocks:
out.sv:5: sorry: constant selects in always_* processes are not currently supported (all bits will be included).
15
out.sv:5: sorry: constant selects in always_* processes are not currently supported (all bits will be included).
